question.text: According to the Supreme Court's findings in the Kanaiyalal Patel case, front-running comprises at least how many forms of conduct?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Supreme Court identified three forms of front-running: (1) tippee trading, (2) self-front-running (hedging against price fluctuations caused by block trade), and (3) trading ahead (intermediary trading ahead of customer block order). **The Trap:** Students may confuse the number of forms with the number of appeal cases discussed. **Cross-Exam Context:** Front-running classification is tested in NISM Series III-A, VIII, and XII exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Know all three forms - tippee trading, self-front-running, and trading ahead - as the exam may test any of them individually.
